etymology.description: 教(教) (jiào, "teach") implies the traditional method of imparting knowledge, involving both explanation and example. 導(導) (dǎo, "guide") conveys the action of leading someone in a direction or showing them the way.
etymology.interpretation: 教(教) (jiào, "teach") involves explaining or showing someone how to do something, often invoking traditional methods or systems of thought. 導(導) (dǎo, "guide") adds the nuance of leading beyond just giving information, suggesting a process of steering someone in learning. Together, they form a comprehensive view of education as both imparting knowledge and guiding through learning journeys.

examples.chinese: 老师教导我们做人要诚实(老師教導我們做人要誠實)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī jiàodǎo wǒmen zuòrén yào chéngshí.
examples.english: The teacher instructs us to be honest.

examples.chinese: 父母的教导影响了他的一生(父母的教導影響了他的一生)。
examples.pinyin: Fùmǔ de jiàodǎo yǐngxiǎngle tā de yīshēng.
examples.english: The instruction from his parents influenced his whole life.

examples.chinese: 在长者的教导下(在長者的教導下)，我们学到了很多宝贵的经验(我們學到了很多寶貴的經驗)。
examples.pinyin: Zài zhǎngzhě de jiàodǎo xià, wǒmen xué dàole hěn duō bǎoguì de jīngyàn.
examples.english: Under the instruction of the elders, we learned many valuable experiences.
